item.title: Public and private markets vie for gains from AI job disruption
item.description: Corporate leaders are betting that automation will produce outsized returns

item.title: Global oil reserves plunge at record pace as Middle East war strains supplies
item.description: Stocks near 8-year low ahead of summer travel season despite collapse in demand

item.title: JPMorgan and BlackRock bosses play down talk of AI bubble
item.description: Dimon and Fink upbeat in separate comments about demand for the technology as Wall Street funds sector’s spending

item.title: Bypassing the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Investing in alternatives to this and other maritime chokepoints is costly but necessary

item.title: Uzbekistan’s London IPO brings frontier markets closer to home
item.description: Resource-rich nation’s move into foreign equity markets could tempt investors with the stomach for risk

item.title: US stock-lending operator charged with $450mn fraud
item.description: Federal prosecutors say Val Sklarov sold shares pledged as collateral for loan

item.title: Why the Iran war might not spur a faster transition to low-carbon energy
item.description: Increased priority on security of power supply might see some countries continue to rely on fossil fuels

item.title: James Murdoch in talks to buy New York Magazine
item.description: Deal would be first major move by Rupert Murdoch’s youngest son since a settlement to exit his father’s media empire

item.title: Crispin Odey accused of seeking to create a ‘false reality’ by UK regulator
item.description: Lawyers for hedge fund boss say that FCA tried to bring sexual harassment case ‘by the back door’

item.title: SEC moves to scrap quarterly reporting requirement
item.description: Wall Street watchdog suggests allowing public companies to file semi-annual reports

item.title: Norway to reopen three gasfields closed down last century
item.description: Move comes as Europe looks to secure more supplies from producers outside the Middle East and Russia

item.title: Meta and Zuckerberg sued by publishers over ‘massive’ copyright infringement
item.description: Tech giant faces lawsuit from five large groups over its use of copyrighted works to train Llama AI models

item.title: Glencore zinc smelter hit by deadly explosion
item.description: Blast at Kazzinc’s facility killed two people and injured five, according to Kazakhstan’s government

item.title: BioNTech to cut fifth of workforce as it winds down Covid vaccine production
item.description: German biotech is shifting towards cancer treatments

item.title: KKR at 50: ‘It used to be easy in the old days’
item.description: As the original private equity barbarian reaches milestone, the FT interviews its founders and their successors

item.title: Defence group rejects short-seller’s IPO disclosure claims as shares slide
item.description: Hunterbrook alleges ammunition maker withheld information from investors

item.title: Nissan to cut 10% of Europe workforce in global restructuring
item.description: Japanese carmaker will also combine its two production lines in UK’s Sunderland plant

item.title: Goldman expands size of management committee
item.description: Bank elevates global heads of M&A and risk for investment banking and trading

item.title: Coinbase to cut 14% of staff over impact of AI and volatile markets
item.description: Crypto exchange’s CEO says the technology is speeding up its processes, meaning fewer employees are needed

item.title: Google, xAI and Microsoft agree to US national security reviews of new AI models
item.description: Agreement with the tech groups follows concerns about Anthropic’s latest Mythos model

item.title: How a beer tax fuelled an Australian debate over energy exports
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, oil surges after US and Iran exchange fire

item.title: Can the meme stock king pull off audacious eBay swoop?
item.description: Ryan Cohen is trying to engineer a $56bn deal to merge video game retailer GameStop with online marketplace eBay

item.title: US bankruptcy fights are heading to London
item.description: The use of foreign venues is the new frontier for debt restructurings at American companies

item.title: Can we make a crazy huge Venn of the BDC ecosystem?
item.description: Up to a point

item.title: AB InBev says ‘cheers to beer’ as it reports first sales rise in 3 years
item.description: World’s largest brewer beats expectations to defy gloom in alcohol sector

item.title: UniCredit reports record quarterly profit as investment income jumps
item.description: Italian leader pressing ahead with €35bn bid for Germany’s Commerzbank

item.title: EQT makes third offer for London-listed Intertek
item.description: Previous bids from Swedish private equity firm were rejected for ‘fundamentally undervaluing’ FTSE 100 company

item.title: Vodafone to take full control of UK mobile operator in £4.3bn deal
item.description: CK Hutchison’s decision to sell 49% stake is latest in series of asset disposals

item.title: Great expectations (for earnings)
item.description: Stocks look cheaper — if everything goes perfectly

item.title: HSBC profits hit by fraud-related credit losses
item.description: First-quarter income falls $100mn to $9.4bn, missing analysts’ expectations
